title: State Police searching for Berkshire County man after suspicious early-morning fire
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: State Police are searching for John Rohane after a suspicious fire at his home in Monterey, Massachusetts.
The early-morning fire at 340 Main Road was determined to be intentionally set, prompting an investigation.
Authorities are searching nearby Beartown State Forest and asking the public to report any sightings of Rohane.

title: Lured by pepperoni, dog rescued from 12-foot backyard sinkhole on Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An 11-year-old Labrador named Tessa was rescued from a 12-foot sinkhole in Falmouth, Cape Cod.
Falmouth Fire and Rescue used pepperoni and a pole leash to safely extract the dog after two hours.
The sinkhole likely formed due to an underground pipe leak that washed away soil beneath the surface.

title: New England-based rescue takes in batch of neglected axolotls
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Libertyland Axolotl Rescue in New England is rehabilitating 16 neglected axolotls with missing limbs and infections.
The nonprofit is the only one in the U.S. focused solely on axolotls, promoting care through education and adoption.
Named after cheeses, the axolotls need donations for vet care and materials for rehabilitation and long-term fostering.

title: The beauty of a few cracks
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein discusses how imperfections symbolize personal growth in her Love Letters newsletter.
A24 film 'The Drama,' starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, offers a preview screening in Somerville.
Readers can win tickets to the screening by submitting questions to the Love Letters column by March 27.

title: Waltham High opens student-run salon for real-world cosmetology training
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Waltham High School opens a cosmetology salon with 25 stations for students to gain practical experience in hair, skin, and nail care.
The student-run salon, which opened on March 20, provides hands-on training required for professional cosmetology licensure.
Public clients can book appointments at the salon where students work under instructor supervision, enhancing the realism of their training.

title: CeCarré brings a $48 gold-and-caviar-topped pizza to the Pru
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: CeCarré Pizza & Provisions opens in the Prudential Center, offering a gold-and-caviar-topped pizza priced at $48.
The Roman-style pizzeria's menu includes specialty pies with toppings like kimchi, hot dogs, and french fries, mostly priced under $30.
CeCarré is a rebranding from Descendant Pizza, with owner Nina Patel emphasizing an elevated pizza menu at the new location.

title: Mass. woman killed in 6-vehicle crash in Florida
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 69-year-old Massachusetts woman died in a six-vehicle crash on I-75 in Ochopee, Florida.
The crash was triggered by a semi-truck failing to slow, causing a chain-reaction collision.
The SUV's driver and another Florida resident were seriously injured; investigation is ongoing.

title: Former Miss Hall’s School teacher at center of grooming allegations is charged with rape
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matthew Rutledge, a former teacher at Miss Hall’s School, was indicted on three counts of rape by a Berkshire County grand jury.
Rutledge is accused of sexually assaulting students while employed as a teacher at the Pittsfield, Massachusetts, prep school.
Former students Melissa Fares and Hilary Simon testified against Rutledge; his arraignment is scheduled for April 22.

title: FIFA reduces hotel room bookings in World Cup cities, including Boston
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: FIFA reduces hotel room bookings in Boston for World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium from June 13 to July 9.
Meet Boston anticipated the reduction due to inflated original estimates and has a strategy to market available rooms.
FIFA is also canceling room bookings in other host cities, affecting operational staff accommodations and hotel revenue forecasts.

title: ‘I had just lost everything’: Kelsey Fitzsimmons testifies in her trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, former North Andover officer, testifies about being shot by a colleague during a restraining order delivery.
Fitzsimmons faces charges of assault and battery with a dangerous weapon after a June 2025 incident involving Officer Patrick Noonan.
Closing arguments in Fitzsimmons's trial are scheduled for Thursday morning in Essex County Superior Court.
